fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Vermont Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Vermont - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity vermont, how to transfer physical therapist license to vermont, physical therapist compact vermont, vermont physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Vermont Office of Professional Regulation — PT
board.mailing_address: Office of Professional Regulation, 89 Main Street, 3rd Floor, Montpelier, VT 05620-3402

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Vermont.

steps.title: Create OPR Online Services Account
steps.description: Navigate to the OPR Online Services Portal and register for a new account using your email address and a chosen password.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your OPR Online Services account, select the Physical Therapist application, and accurately complete all required sections, including personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Required Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your accredited physical therapy program and official NPTE scores to be sent directly to the Office of Professional Regulation. Upload any other necessary supporting documents as prompted within the OPR Online Services Portal.

steps.title: Pass Jurisprudence Examination
steps.description: Successfully complete the Vermont Physical Therapy Jurisprudence Examination, which is typically accessed through or linked from the online application system.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable application fee of $100 through the OPR Online Services Portal using a credit or debit card.

steps.title: Await Review and Issuance
steps.description: Once all required items are received and reviewed by the Office of Professional Regulation, your application will be processed, and you will be notified of your licensure status.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Vermont physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Vermont have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Vermont participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Vermont without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Vermont?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Vermont while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Vermont
hero.dek: Apply for your Physical Therapist license through the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation's OPR Online Services Portal.
